---
title: "Binomial Distribution"
subtitle: "SE01"
toc: True
---

## Overview

This session introduces the binomial distribution through an epidemiological example.


::: {.callout-note}
## Learning Objectives
By the end of this session, you will be able to:

- Explain the assumptions of the binomial distribution.

- Interpret a simple epidemiological example.

- Read code used to calculate binomial probabilities.

- Interpret a visualisation of the distribution.

:::










## Summary
*Session summary placeholder*

<!-- START_NAVIGATION -->


```{=html}
<div class="custom-page-navigation">
    <div class="nav-prev">
        
        <a href="../index.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">← Previous</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Binomial Distribution in Epidemiology</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
    <div class="nav-next">
        
        <a href="01-core-concepts-and-interpretation/index.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">Next →</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Core Concepts and Interpretation</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
</div>
```

<!-- END_NAVIGATION -->
